title: Poached Russian Pike-perch Pilaf
description: A kid-friendly, macrobiotic-friendly pilaf dish featuring poached pike-perch, crispy onions, and a tangy balsamic glaze.
instructions: Poach the pike-perch fillets in a large pot of simmering water for 8 minutes, or until cooked through.
Prepare the pilaf by heating the olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the diced onion to the skillet and cook, stirring occasionally, until crispy and golden brown, about 5-7 minutes.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook for an additional minute, stirring constantly.
Add the cooked rice to the skillet and stir to combine with the onion and garlic mixture.
Season the pilaf with salt and black pepper to taste.
To assemble the pilaf, place a portion of the cooked rice mixture onto a plate, top with a poached pike-perch fillet, and drizzle with balsamic glaze.
To make the balsamic glaze, reduce 2 tbsp of balsamic vinegar on the stovetop until thick and syrupy, stirring occasionally.
